modules/cache/cache_storage.c

2 * contributor license agreements.  See the NOTICE file distributed with
3 * this work for additional information regarding copyright ownership.

5 * (the "License"); you may not use this file except in compliance with
6 * the License.  You may obtain a copy of the License at
7 *
8 *     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
9 *
10 * Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
11 * distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
12 * WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
13 * See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
14 * limitations under the License.
15 */
16
17#include "mod_cache.h"
18
19#include "cache_storage.h"
20#include "cache_util.h"
21
22APLOG_USE_MODULE(cache);
23
24extern APR_OPTIONAL_FN_TYPE(ap_cache_generate_key) *cache_generate_key;
25
26extern module AP_MODULE_DECLARE_DATA cache_module;
27
28/* -------------------------------------------------------------- */ view japanese
29
30/*
31 * delete all URL entities from the cache
32 *
33 */ view japanese
34int cache_remove_url(cache_request_rec *cache, request_rec *r)
35{
36    cache_provider_list *list;
37    cache_handle_t *h;
38
39    list = cache->providers;
40
41    /* Remove the stale cache entry if present. If not, we're
42     * being called from outside of a request; remove the
43     * non-stale handle.
44     */ view japanese
45    h = cache->stale_handle ? cache->stale_handle : cache->handle;
46    if (!h) {
47       return OK;
48    }
49    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, 0, r, APLOGNO(00691)
50                 "cache: Removing url %s from the cache", h->cache_obj->key);
51
52    /* for each specified cache type, delete the URL */
53    while (list) {
54        list->provider->remove_url(h, r);
55        list = list->next;
56    }
57    return OK;
58}
59
60
61/*
62 * create a new URL entity in the cache
63 *
64 * It is possible to store more than once entity per URL. This
65 * function will always create a new entity, regardless of whether
66 * other entities already exist for the same URL.
67 *
68 * The size of the entity is provided so that a cache module can
69 * decide whether or not it wants to cache this particular entity.
70 * If the size is unknown, a size of -1 should be set.
71 */ view japanese
72int cache_create_entity(cache_request_rec *cache, request_rec *r,
73                        apr_off_t size, apr_bucket_brigade *in)
74{
75    cache_provider_list *list;
76    cache_handle_t *h = apr_pcalloc(r->pool, sizeof(cache_handle_t));
77    apr_status_t rv;
78
79    if (!cache) {
80        /* This should never happen */
81        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, APR_EGENERAL, r, APLOGNO(00692)
82                "cache: No cache request information available for key"
83                " generation");
84        return APR_EGENERAL;
85    }
86
87    if (!cache->key) {
88        rv = cache_generate_key(r, r->pool, &cache->key);
89        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
90            return rv;
91        }
92    }
93
94    list = cache->providers;
95    /* for each specified cache type, delete the URL */ view japanese
96    while (list) {
97        switch (rv = list->provider->create_entity(h, r, cache->key, size, in)) {
98        case OK: {
99            cache->handle = h;
100            cache->provider = list->provider;
101            cache->provider_name = list->provider_name;
102            return OK;
103        }
104        case DECLINED: {
105            list = list->next;
106            continue;
107        }
108        default: {
109            return rv;
110        }
111        }
112    }
113    return DECLINED;
114}
115
116static int filter_header_do(void *v, const char *key, const char *val)
117{
118    if ((*key == 'W' || *key == 'w') && !ap_cstr_casecmp(key, "Warning")
119            && *val == '1') {
120        /* any stored Warning headers with warn-code 1xx (see section
121         * 14.46) MUST be deleted from the cache entry and the forwarded
122         * response.
123         */ view japanese
124    }
125    else {
126        apr_table_addn(v, key, val);
127    }
128    return 1;
129}
130static int remove_header_do(void *v, const char *key, const char *val)
131{
132    if ((*key == 'W' || *key == 'w') && !ap_cstr_casecmp(key, "Warning")) {
133        /* any stored Warning headers with warn-code 2xx MUST be retained
134         * in the cache entry and the forwarded response.
135         */ view japanese
136    }
137    else {
138        apr_table_unset(v, key);
139    }
140    return 1;
141}
142static int add_header_do(void *v, const char *key, const char *val)
143{
144    apr_table_addn(v, key, val);
145    return 1;
146}
147
148/**
149 * Take two sets of headers, sandwich them together, and apply the result to
150 * r->headers_out.
151 *
152 * To complicate this, a header may be duplicated in either table. Should a
153 * header exist in the top table, all matching headers will be removed from
154 * the bottom table before the headers are combined. The Warning headers are
155 * handled specially. Warnings are added rather than being replaced, while
156 * in the case of revalidation 1xx Warnings are stripped.
157 *
158 * The Content-Type and Last-Modified headers are then re-parsed and inserted
159 * into the request.
160 */ view japanese
161void cache_accept_headers(cache_handle_t *h, request_rec *r, apr_table_t *top,
162        apr_table_t *bottom, int revalidation)
163{
164    const char *v;
165
166    if (revalidation) {
167        r->headers_out = apr_table_make(r->pool, 10);
168        apr_table_do(filter_header_do, r->headers_out, bottom, NULL);
169    }
170    else if (r->headers_out != bottom) {
171        r->headers_out = apr_table_copy(r->pool, bottom);
172    }
173    apr_table_do(remove_header_do, r->headers_out, top, NULL);
174    apr_table_do(add_header_do, r->headers_out, top, NULL);
175
176    v = apr_table_get(r->headers_out, "Content-Type");
177    if (v) {
178        ap_set_content_type(r, v);
179        /*
180         * Also unset possible Content-Type headers in r->headers_out and
181         * r->err_headers_out as they may be different to what we have received
182         * from the cache.
183         * Actually they are not needed as r->content_type set by
184         * ap_set_content_type above will be used in the store_headers functions
185         * of the storage providers as a fallback and the HTTP_HEADER filter
186         * does overwrite the Content-Type header with r->content_type anyway.
187         */ view japanese
188        apr_table_unset(r->headers_out, "Content-Type");
189        apr_table_unset(r->err_headers_out, "Content-Type");
190    }
191
192    /* If the cache gave us a Last-Modified header, we can't just
193     * pass it on blindly because of restrictions on future values.
194     */ view japanese
195    v = apr_table_get(r->headers_out, "Last-Modified");
196    if (v) {
197        ap_update_mtime(r, apr_date_parse_http(v));
198        ap_set_last_modified(r);
199    }
200
201}
202
203/*
204 * select a specific URL entity in the cache
205 *
206 * It is possible to store more than one entity per URL. Content
207 * negotiation is used to select an entity. Once an entity is
208 * selected, details of it are stored in the per request
209 * config to save time when serving the request later.
210 *
211 * This function returns OK if successful, DECLINED if no
212 * cached entity fits the bill.
213 */ view japanese
214int cache_select(cache_request_rec *cache, request_rec *r)
215{
216    cache_provider_list *list;
217    apr_status_t rv;
218    cache_handle_t *h;
219
220    if (!cache) {
221        /* This should never happen */
222        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, APR_EGENERAL, r, APLOGNO(00693)
223                "cache: No cache request information available for key"
224                " generation");
225        return DECLINED;
226    }
227
228    /* if no-cache, we can't serve from the cache, but we may store to the
229     * cache.
230     */ view japanese
231    if (!ap_cache_check_no_cache(cache, r)) {
232        return DECLINED;
233    }
234
235    if (!cache->key) {
236        rv = cache_generate_key(r, r->pool, &cache->key);
237        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
238            return DECLINED;
239        }
240    }
241
242    /* go through the cache types till we get a match */ view japanese
243    h = apr_palloc(r->pool, sizeof(cache_handle_t));
244
245    list = cache->providers;
246
247    while (list) {
248        switch ((rv = list->provider->open_entity(h, r, cache->key))) {
249        case OK: {
250            char *vary = NULL;
251            int mismatch = 0;
252            char *last = NULL;
253
254            if (list->provider->recall_headers(h, r) != APR_SUCCESS) {
255                /* try again with next cache type */
256                list = list->next;
257                continue;
258            }
259
260            /*
261             * Check Content-Negotiation - Vary
262             *
263             * At this point we need to make sure that the object we found in
264             * the cache is the same object that would be delivered to the
265             * client, when the effects of content negotiation are taken into
266             * effect.
267             *
268             * In plain english, we want to make sure that a language-negotiated
269             * document in one language is not given to a client asking for a
270             * language negotiated document in a different language by mistake.
271             *
272             * This code makes the assumption that the storage manager will
273             * cache the req_hdrs if the response contains a Vary header.
274             *
275             * RFC2616 13.6 and 14.44 describe the Vary mechanism.
276             */ view japanese
277            vary = cache_strqtok(
278                    apr_pstrdup(r->pool,
279                            cache_table_getm(r->pool, h->resp_hdrs, "Vary")),
280                    CACHE_SEPARATOR, &last);
281            while (vary) {
282                const char *h1, *h2;
283
284                /*
285                 * is this header in the request and the header in the cached
286                 * request identical? If not, we give up and do a straight get
287                 */ view japanese
288                h1 = cache_table_getm(r->pool, r->headers_in, vary);
289                h2 = cache_table_getm(r->pool, h->req_hdrs, vary);
290                if (h1 == h2) {
291                    /* both headers NULL, so a match - do nothing */ view japanese
292                }
293                else if (h1 && h2 && !strcmp(h1, h2)) {
294                    /* both headers exist and are equal - do nothing */ view japanese
295                }
296                else {
297                    /* headers do not match, so Vary failed */ view japanese
298                    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, APR_SUCCESS,
299                            r, APLOGNO(00694) "cache_select(): Vary header mismatch.");
300                    mismatch = 1;
301                    break;
302                }
303                vary = cache_strqtok(NULL, CACHE_SEPARATOR, &last);
304            }
305
306            /* no vary match, try next provider */ view japanese
307            if (mismatch) {
308                /* try again with next cache type */
309                list = list->next;
310                continue;
311            }
312
313            cache->provider = list->provider;
314            cache->provider_name = list->provider_name;
315
316            /*
317             * RFC2616 13.3.4 Rules for When to Use Entity Tags and Last-Modified
318             * Dates: An HTTP/1.1 caching proxy, upon receiving a conditional request
319             * that includes both a Last-Modified date and one or more entity tags as
320             * cache validators, MUST NOT return a locally cached response to the
321             * client unless that cached response is consistent with all of the
322             * conditional header fields in the request.
323             */ view japanese
324            if (ap_condition_if_match(r, h->resp_hdrs) == AP_CONDITION_NOMATCH
325                    || ap_condition_if_unmodified_since(r, h->resp_hdrs)
326                            == AP_CONDITION_NOMATCH
327                    || ap_condition_if_none_match(r, h->resp_hdrs)
328                            == AP_CONDITION_NOMATCH
329                    || ap_condition_if_modified_since(r, h->resp_hdrs)
330                            == AP_CONDITION_NOMATCH
331                    || ap_condition_if_range(r, h->resp_hdrs) == AP_CONDITION_NOMATCH) {
332                mismatch = 1;
333            }
334
335            /* Is our cached response fresh enough? */ view japanese
336            if (mismatch || !cache_check_freshness(h, cache, r)) {
337                const char *etag, *lastmod;
338
339                /* Cache-Control: only-if-cached and revalidation required, try
340                 * the next provider
341                 */ view japanese
342                if (cache->control_in.only_if_cached) {
343                    /* try again with next cache type */
344                    list = list->next;
345                    continue;
346                }
347
348                /* set aside the stale entry for accessing later */ view japanese
349                cache->stale_headers = apr_table_copy(r->pool,
350                        r->headers_in);
351                cache->stale_handle = h;
352
353                /* if no existing conditionals, use conditionals of our own */ view japanese
354                if (!mismatch) {
355
356                    ap_log_rerror(
357                            APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, APR_SUCCESS, r, APLOGNO(00695) "Cached response for %s isn't fresh. Adding "
358                            "conditional request headers.", r->uri);
359
360                    /* Remove existing conditionals that might conflict with ours */ view japanese
361                    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Match");
362                    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Modified-Since");
363                    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-None-Match");
364                    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Range");
365                    apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "If-Unmodified-Since");
366
367                    etag = apr_table_get(h->resp_hdrs, "ETag");
368                    lastmod = apr_table_get(h->resp_hdrs, "Last-Modified");
369
370                    if (etag || lastmod) {
371                        /* If we have a cached etag and/or Last-Modified add in
372                         * our own conditionals.
373                         */ view japanese
374
375                        if (etag) {
376                            apr_table_set(r->headers_in, "If-None-Match", etag);
377                        }
378
379                        if (lastmod) {
380                            apr_table_set(r->headers_in, "If-Modified-Since",
381                                    lastmod);
382                        }
383
384                        /*
385                         * Do not do Range requests with our own conditionals: If
386                         * we get 304 the Range does not matter and otherwise the
387                         * entity changed and we want to have the complete entity
388                         */ view japanese
389                        apr_table_unset(r->headers_in, "Range");
390
391                    }
392
393                }
394
395                /* ready to revalidate, pretend we were never here */ view japanese
396                return DECLINED;
397            }
398
399            /* Okay, this response looks okay.  Merge in our stuff and go. */ view japanese
400            cache_accept_headers(h, r, h->resp_hdrs, r->headers_out, 0);
401
402            cache->handle = h;
403            return OK;
404        }
405        case DECLINED: {
406            /* try again with next cache type */ view japanese
407            list = list->next;
408            continue;
409        }
410        default: {
411            /* oo-er! an error */ view japanese
412            return rv;
413        }
414        }
415    }
416
417    /* if Cache-Control: only-if-cached, and not cached, return 504 */ view japanese
418    if (cache->control_in.only_if_cached) {
419        ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, APR_SUCCESS, r, APLOGNO(00696)
420                "cache: 'only-if-cached' requested and no cached entity, "
421                "returning 504 Gateway Timeout for: %s", r->uri);
422        return HTTP_GATEWAY_TIME_OUT;
423    }
424
425    return DECLINED;
426}
427
428static apr_status_t cache_canonicalise_key(request_rec *r, apr_pool_t* p,
429                                           const char *path, const char *query,
430                                           apr_uri_t *parsed_uri,
431                                           const char **key)
432{
433    cache_server_conf *conf;
434    char *port_str, *hn, *lcs;
435    const char *hostname, *scheme;
436    int i;
437    const char *kpath;
438    const char *kquery;
439
440    if (*key) {
441        /*
442         * We have been here before during the processing of this request.
443         */ view japanese
444        return APR_SUCCESS;
445    }
446
447    /*
448     * Get the module configuration. We need this for the CacheIgnoreQueryString
449     * option below.
450     */ view japanese
451    conf = (cache_server_conf *) ap_get_module_config(r->server->module_config,
452            &cache_module);
453
454    /*
455     * Use the canonical name to improve cache hit rate, but only if this is
456     * not a proxy request or if this is a reverse proxy request.
457     * We need to handle both cases in the same manner as for the reverse proxy
458     * case we have the following situation:
459     *
460     * If a cached entry is looked up by mod_cache's quick handler r->proxyreq
461     * is still unset in the reverse proxy case as it only gets set in the
462     * translate name hook (either by ProxyPass or mod_rewrite) which is run
463     * after the quick handler hook. This is different to the forward proxy
464     * case where it gets set before the quick handler is run (in the
465     * post_read_request hook).
466     * If a cache entry is created by the CACHE_SAVE filter we always have
467     * r->proxyreq set correctly.
468     * So we must ensure that in the reverse proxy case we use the same code
469     * path and using the canonical name seems to be the right thing to do
470     * in the reverse proxy case.
471     */ view japanese
472    if (!r->proxyreq || (r->proxyreq == PROXYREQ_REVERSE)) {
473        if (conf->base_uri && conf->base_uri->hostname) {
474            hostname = conf->base_uri->hostname;
475        }
476        else {
477            /* Use _default_ as the hostname if none present, as in mod_vhost */ view japanese
478            hostname = ap_get_server_name(r);
479            if (!hostname) {
480                hostname = "_default_";
481            }
482        }
483    }
484    else if (parsed_uri->hostname) {
485        /* Copy the parsed uri hostname */ view japanese
486        hn = apr_pstrdup(p, parsed_uri->hostname);
487        ap_str_tolower(hn);
488        /* const work-around */
489        hostname = hn;
490    }
491    else {
492        /* We are a proxied request, with no hostname. Unlikely
493         * to get very far - but just in case */ view japanese
494        hostname = "_default_";
495    }
496
497    /*
498     * Copy the scheme, ensuring that it is lower case. If the parsed uri
499     * contains no string or if this is not a proxy request get the http
500     * scheme for this request. As r->parsed_uri.scheme is not set if this
501     * is a reverse proxy request, it is ensured that the cases
502     * "no proxy request" and "reverse proxy request" are handled in the same
503     * manner (see above why this is needed).
504     */ view japanese
505    if (r->proxyreq && parsed_uri->scheme) {
506        /* Copy the scheme and lower-case it */ view japanese
507        lcs = apr_pstrdup(p, parsed_uri->scheme);
508        ap_str_tolower(lcs);
509        /* const work-around */ view japanese
510        scheme = lcs;
511    }
512    else {
513        if (conf->base_uri && conf->base_uri->scheme) {
514            scheme = conf->base_uri->scheme;
515        }
516        else {
517            scheme = ap_http_scheme(r);
518        }
519    }
520
521    /*
522     * If this is a proxy request, but not a reverse proxy request (see comment
523     * above why these cases must be handled in the same manner), copy the
524     * URI's port-string (which may be a service name). If the URI contains
525     * no port-string, use apr-util's notion of the default port for that
526     * scheme - if available. Otherwise use the port-number of the current
527     * server.
528     */ view japanese
529    if (r->proxyreq && (r->proxyreq != PROXYREQ_REVERSE)) {
530        if (parsed_uri->port_str) {
531            port_str = apr_pcalloc(p, strlen(parsed_uri->port_str) + 2);
532            port_str[0] = ':';
533            for (i = 0; parsed_uri->port_str[i]; i++) {
534                port_str[i + 1] = apr_tolower(parsed_uri->port_str[i]);
535            }
536        }
537        else if (apr_uri_port_of_scheme(scheme)) {
538            port_str = apr_psprintf(p, ":%u", apr_uri_port_of_scheme(scheme));
539        }
540        else {
541            /* No port string given in the AbsoluteUri, and we have no
542             * idea what the default port for the scheme is. Leave it
543             * blank and live with the inefficiency of some extra cached
544             * entities.
545             */ view japanese
546            port_str = "";
547        }
548    }
549    else {
550        if (conf->base_uri && conf->base_uri->port_str) {
551            port_str = apr_pstrcat(p, ":", conf->base_uri->port_str, NULL);
552        }
553        else if (conf->base_uri && conf->base_uri->hostname) {
554            port_str = "";
555        }
556        else {
557            /* Use the server port */ view japanese
558            port_str = apr_psprintf(p, ":%u", ap_get_server_port(r));
559        }
560    }
561
562    /*
563     * Check if we need to ignore session identifiers in the URL and do so
564     * if needed.
565     */ view japanese
566    kpath = path;
567    kquery = conf->ignorequerystring ? NULL : query;
568    if (conf->ignore_session_id->nelts) {
569        int i;
570        char **identifier;
571
572        identifier = (char **) conf->ignore_session_id->elts;
573        for (i = 0; i < conf->ignore_session_id->nelts; i++, identifier++) {
574            int len;
575            const char *param;
576
577            len = strlen(*identifier);
578            /*
579             * Check that we have a parameter separator in the last segment
580             * of the path and that the parameter matches our identifier
581             */ view japanese
582            if ((param = ap_strrchr_c(kpath, ';'))
583                    && !strncmp(param + 1, *identifier, len)
584                    && (*(param + len + 1) == '=')
585                    && !ap_strchr_c(param + len + 2, '/')) {
586                kpath = apr_pstrmemdup(p, kpath, param - kpath);
587                continue;
588            }
589            /*
590             * Check if the identifier is in the query string and cut it out.
591             */ view japanese
592            if (kquery && *kquery) {
593                /*
594                 * First check if the identifier is at the beginning of the
595                 * query string and followed by a '='
596                 */ view japanese
597                if (!strncmp(kquery, *identifier, len) && kquery[len] == '=') {
598                    param = kquery;
599                }
600                else {
601                    char *complete;
602
603                    /*
604                     * In order to avoid subkey matching (PR 48401) prepend
605                     * identifier with a '&' and append a '='
606                     */ view japanese
607                    complete = apr_pstrcat(p, "&", *identifier, "=", NULL);
608                    param = ap_strstr_c(kquery, complete);
609                    /* If we found something we are sitting on the '&' */ view japanese
610                    if (param) {
611                        param++;
612                    }
613                }
614                if (param) {
615                    const char *amp;
616                    char *dup = NULL;
617
618                    if (kquery != param) {
619                        dup = apr_pstrmemdup(p, kquery, param - kquery);
620                        kquery = dup;
621                    }
622                    else {
623                        kquery = "";
624                    }
625
626                    if ((amp = ap_strchr_c(param + len + 1, '&'))) {
627                        kquery = apr_pstrcat(p, kquery, amp + 1, NULL);
628                    }
629                    else {
630                        /*
631                         * If query string is not "", then we have the case
632                         * that the identifier parameter we removed was the
633                         * last one in the original query string. Hence we have
634                         * a trailing '&' which needs to be removed.
635                         */ view japanese
636                        if (dup) {
637                            dup[strlen(dup) - 1] = '\0';
638                        }
639                    }
640                }
641            }
642        }
643    }
644
645    /* Key format is a URI, optionally without the query-string (NULL
646     * per above if conf->ignorequerystring)
647     */ view japanese
648    *key = apr_pstrcat(p, scheme, "://", hostname, port_str, view japanese
649                       kpath, "?", kquery, NULL);
650
651    /*
652     * Store the key in the request_config for the cache as r->parsed_uri
653     * might have changed in the time from our first visit here triggered by the
654     * quick handler and our possible second visit triggered by the CACHE_SAVE
655     * filter (e.g. r->parsed_uri got unescaped). In this case we would save the
656     * resource in the cache under a key where it is never found by the quick
657     * handler during following requests.
658     */ view japanese
659    ap_log_rerror(APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, APR_SUCCESS, r, APLOGNO(00698)
660                  "cache: Key for entity %s?%s is %s", path, query, *key);
661
662    return APR_SUCCESS;
663}
664
665apr_status_t cache_generate_key_default(request_rec *r, apr_pool_t* p,
666                                        const char **key)
667{
668    /* In early processing (quick-handler, forward proxy), we want the initial
669     * query-string from r->parsed_uri, since any change before CACHE_SAVE
670     * shouldn't modify the key. Otherwise we want the actual query-string.
671     */ view japanese
672    const char *path = r->uri;
673    const char *query = r->args;
674    if (cache_use_early_url(r)) {
675        path = r->parsed_uri.path;
676        query = r->parsed_uri.query;
677    }
678    return cache_canonicalise_key(r, p, path, query, &r->parsed_uri, key);
679}
680
681/*
682 * Invalidate a specific URL entity in all caches
683 *
684 * All cached entities for this URL are removed, usually in
685 * response to a POST/PUT or DELETE.
686 *
687 * This function returns OK if at least one entity was found and
688 * removed, and DECLINED if no cached entities were removed.
689 */ view japanese
690int cache_invalidate(cache_request_rec *cache, request_rec *r)
691{
692    cache_provider_list *list;
693    apr_status_t rv, status = DECLINED;
694    cache_handle_t *h;
695    apr_uri_t location_uri;
696    apr_uri_t content_location_uri;
697
698    const char *location, *location_key = NULL;
699    const char *content_location, *content_location_key = NULL;
700
701    if (!cache) {
702        /* This should never happen */ view japanese
703        ap_log_rerror(
704                APLOG_MARK, APLOG_ERR, APR_EGENERAL, r, APLOGNO(00697) "cache: No cache request information available for key"
705                " generation");
706        return DECLINED;
707    }
708
709    if (!cache->key) {
710        rv = cache_generate_key(r, r->pool, &cache->key);
711        if (rv != APR_SUCCESS) {
712            return DECLINED;
713        }
714    }
715
716    location = apr_table_get(r->headers_out, "Location");
717    if (location) {
718        if (apr_uri_parse(r->pool, location, &location_uri)
719                || cache_canonicalise_key(r, r->pool,
720                                          location_uri.path,
721                                          location_uri.query,
722                                          &location_uri, &location_key)
723                || !(r->parsed_uri.hostname
724                     && location_uri.hostname
725                     && !strcmp(r->parsed_uri.hostname,
726                                location_uri.hostname))) {
727            location_key = NULL;
728        }
729    }
730
731    content_location = apr_table_get(r->headers_out, "Content-Location");
732    if (content_location) {
733        if (apr_uri_parse(r->pool, content_location,
734                          &content_location_uri)
735                || cache_canonicalise_key(r, r->pool,
736                                          content_location_uri.path,
737                                          content_location_uri.query,
738                                          &content_location_uri,
739                                          &content_location_key)
740                || !(r->parsed_uri.hostname
741                     && content_location_uri.hostname
742                     && !strcmp(r->parsed_uri.hostname,
743                                content_location_uri.hostname))) {
744            content_location_key = NULL;
745        }
746    }
747
748    /* go through the cache types */ view japanese
749    h = apr_palloc(r->pool, sizeof(cache_handle_t));
750
751    list = cache->providers;
752
753    while (list) {
754
755        /* invalidate the request uri */ view japanese
756        rv = list->provider->open_entity(h, r, cache->key);
757        if (OK == rv) {
758            rv = list->provider->invalidate_entity(h, r);
759            status = OK;
760        }
761        ap_log_rerror(
762                APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, r, APLOGNO(02468) "cache: Attempted to invalidate cached entity with key: %s", cache->key);
763
764        /* invalidate the Location */ view japanese
765        if (location_key) {
766            rv = list->provider->open_entity(h, r, location_key);
767            if (OK == rv) {
768                rv = list->provider->invalidate_entity(h, r);
769                status = OK;
770            }
771            ap_log_rerror(
772                    APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, r, APLOGNO(02469) "cache: Attempted to invalidate cached entity with key: %s", location_key);
773        }
774
775        /* invalidate the Content-Location */ view japanese
776        if (content_location_key) {
777            rv = list->provider->open_entity(h, r, content_location_key);
778            if (OK == rv) {
779                rv = list->provider->invalidate_entity(h, r);
780                status = OK;
781            }
782            ap_log_rerror(
783                    APLOG_MARK, APLOG_DEBUG, rv, r, APLOGNO(02470) "cache: Attempted to invalidate cached entity with key: %s", content_location_key);
784        }
785
786        list = list->next;
787    }
788
789    return status;
790}

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:28

/* -------------------------------------------------------------- */

Original comment

/* -------------------------------------------------------------- */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:30-33

/*
 * キャッシュからすべてのURLエンティティを削除する
 *
 */

Original comment

/*
 * delete all URL entities from the cache
 *
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:41-44

/* 古いキャッシュエントリが存在する場合は削除する。存在しない場合は、
     * リクエスト外から呼び出されているため、古いものではないハンドルを削除する。
     */

Original comment

/* Remove the stale cache entry if present. If not, we're
     * being called from outside of a request; remove the
     * non-stale handle.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:95

/* 指定された各キャッシュタイプについて、URLを削除する */

Original comment

/* for each specified cache type, delete the URL */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:61-71

/*
 * キャッシュ内に新しいURLエンティティを作成する
 *
 * 1つのURLに対して複数のエンティティを格納することが可能です。この
 * 関数は、同じURLに対して他のエンティティが既に存在するかどうかにかかわらず、
 * 常に新しいエンティティを作成します。
 *
 * エンティティのサイズが提供されるのは、キャッシュモジュールがその特定の
 * エンティティをキャッシュするかどうかを判断できるようにするためです。
 * サイズが不明な場合は、-1を設定する必要があります。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * create a new URL entity in the cache
 *
 * It is possible to store more than once entity per URL. This
 * function will always create a new entity, regardless of whether
 * other entities already exist for the same URL.
 *
 * The size of the entity is provided so that a cache module can
 * decide whether or not it wants to cache this particular entity.
 * If the size is unknown, a size of -1 should be set.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:702

/* これは本来起こり得ないはずです */

Original comment

/* This should never happen */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:120-123

/* warn-code 1xx の保存されている Warning ヘッダー（セクション 14.46 を参照）は、
         * キャッシュエントリおよび転送されるレスポンスから削除しなければならない（MUST）。
         */

Original comment

/* any stored Warning headers with warn-code 1xx (see section
         * 14.46) MUST be deleted from the cache entry and the forwarded
         * response.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:133-135

/* warn-code 2xx を持つ保存済みの Warning ヘッダーは、
         * キャッシュエントリおよび転送されるレスポンス内に保持しなければならない (MUST)。
         */

Original comment

/* any stored Warning headers with warn-code 2xx MUST be retained
         * in the cache entry and the forwarded response.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:148-160

/**
 * 2つのヘッダーセットを受け取り、それらを結合して結果を r->headers_out に適用する。
 *
 * 複雑な点として、ヘッダーがどちらのテーブルにも重複して存在する可能性がある。
 * 上側のテーブルにヘッダーが存在する場合、ヘッダーを結合する前に、下側のテーブルから一致するすべてのヘッダーが削除される。
 * Warning ヘッダーは特別に扱われる。Warning は置換されるのではなく追加されるが、再検証（revalidation）の場合は 1xx Warning は削除される。
 *
 * その後、Content-Type ヘッダーと Last-Modified ヘッダーが再解析され、リクエストに挿入される。
 */

Original comment

/**
 * Take two sets of headers, sandwich them together, and apply the result to
 * r->headers_out.
 *
 * To complicate this, a header may be duplicated in either table. Should a
 * header exist in the top table, all matching headers will be removed from
 * the bottom table before the headers are combined. The Warning headers are
 * handled specially. Warnings are added rather than being replaced, while
 * in the case of revalidation 1xx Warnings are stripped.
 *
 * The Content-Type and Last-Modified headers are then re-parsed and inserted
 * into the request.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:179-187

/*
         * r->headers_out および r->err_headers_out に存在する可能性のある Content-Type ヘッダーも、
         * キャッシュから受け取ったものと異なる可能性があるため、設定を解除します。
         * 実際には、上記で ap_set_content_type によって設定された r->content_type が
         * ストレージプロバイダーの store_headers 関数でフォールバックとして使用され、
         * HTTP_HEADER フィルターがいずれにせよ Content-Type ヘッダーを r->content_type で
         * 上書きするため、これらは不要です。
         */

Original comment

/*
         * Also unset possible Content-Type headers in r->headers_out and
         * r->err_headers_out as they may be different to what we have received
         * from the cache.
         * Actually they are not needed as r->content_type set by
         * ap_set_content_type above will be used in the store_headers functions
         * of the storage providers as a fallback and the HTTP_HEADER filter
         * does overwrite the Content-Type header with r->content_type anyway.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:192-194

/* キャッシュがLast-Modifiedヘッダーを提供した場合、将来の日付に関する制限があるため、そのまま盲目的に渡すことはできません。
     */

Original comment

/* If the cache gave us a Last-Modified header, we can't just
     * pass it on blindly because of restrictions on future values.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:203-213

/*
 * キャッシュ内の特定のURLエンティティを選択する
 *
 * 1つのURLに対して複数のエンティティを格納することが可能です。
 * コンテンツネゴシエーションを使用してエンティティを選択します。
 * エンティティが選択されると、後でリクエストを処理する際の時間を節約するため、
 * その詳細がリクエストごとの設定に保存されます。
 *
 * この関数は、成功した場合はOKを返し、適合するキャッシュ済みエンティティがない場合はDECLINEDを返します。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * select a specific URL entity in the cache
 *
 * It is possible to store more than one entity per URL. Content
 * negotiation is used to select an entity. Once an entity is
 * selected, details of it are stored in the per request
 * config to save time when serving the request later.
 *
 * This function returns OK if successful, DECLINED if no
 * cached entity fits the bill.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:228-230

/* no-cache の場合、キャッシュから提供することはできませんが、キャッシュに保存することは可能です。
     */

Original comment

/* if no-cache, we can't serve from the cache, but we may store to the
     * cache.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:242

/* 一致するものが見つかるまでキャッシュタイプを順に確認する */

Original comment

/* go through the cache types till we get a match */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:406

/* 次のキャッシュタイプで再試行 */

Original comment

/* try again with next cache type */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:260-276

/*
             * Content-Negotiation - Vary の確認
             *
             * この時点で、キャッシュ内で見つかったオブジェクトが、コンテンツネゴシエーションの結果を考慮した上でクライアントに配信されるオブジェクトと同一であることを確認する必要があります。
             *
             * 簡単に言えば、ある言語でネゴシエーションされたドキュメントが、誤って別の言語でのネゴシエーションを要求しているクライアントに提供されないようにする必要があります。
             *
             * このコードは、レスポンスに Vary ヘッダーが含まれている場合、ストレージマネージャーが req_hdrs をキャッシュするという前提に基づいています。
             *
             * RFC2616 13.6 および 14.44 で Vary メカニズムについて説明されています。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * Check Content-Negotiation - Vary
             *
             * At this point we need to make sure that the object we found in
             * the cache is the same object that would be delivered to the
             * client, when the effects of content negotiation are taken into
             * effect.
             *
             * In plain english, we want to make sure that a language-negotiated
             * document in one language is not given to a client asking for a
             * language negotiated document in a different language by mistake.
             *
             * This code makes the assumption that the storage manager will
             * cache the req_hdrs if the response contains a Vary header.
             *
             * RFC2616 13.6 and 14.44 describe the Vary mechanism.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:284-287

/*
                 * リクエスト内のこのヘッダーとキャッシュされたリクエスト内のヘッダーは同一か？
                 * 同一でなければ、諦めて通常のGETを行う
                 */

Original comment

/*
                 * is this header in the request and the header in the cached
                 * request identical? If not, we give up and do a straight get
                 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:291

/* 両方のヘッダーが NULL なので一致とみなす - 何もしない */

Original comment

/* both headers NULL, so a match - do nothing */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:294

/* 両方のヘッダーが存在し、かつ等しい - 何もしない */

Original comment

/* both headers exist and are equal - do nothing */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:297

/* ヘッダーが一致しないため、Vary は失敗しました */

Original comment

/* headers do not match, so Vary failed */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:306

/* varyの一致なし、次のプロバイダーを試行 */

Original comment

/* no vary match, try next provider */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:316-323

/*
             * RFC2616 13.3.4 エンティティタグおよびLast-Modified日付を使用する場合のルール: 
             * HTTP/1.1 キャッシュプロキシは、キャッシュバリデーターとしてLast-Modified日付と1つ以上のエンティティタグの両方を含む条件付きリクエストを受信した場合、
             * そのキャッシュされたレスポンスがリクエスト内のすべての条件付きヘッダーフィールドと整合しない限り、
             * ローカルにキャッシュされたレスポンスをクライアントに返してはならない（MUST NOT）。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * RFC2616 13.3.4 Rules for When to Use Entity Tags and Last-Modified
             * Dates: An HTTP/1.1 caching proxy, upon receiving a conditional request
             * that includes both a Last-Modified date and one or more entity tags as
             * cache validators, MUST NOT return a locally cached response to the
             * client unless that cached response is consistent with all of the
             * conditional header fields in the request.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:335

/* キャッシュされたレスポンスは十分に新しいか？ */

Original comment

/* Is our cached response fresh enough? */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:339-341

/* Cache-Control: only-if-cached かつ再検証が必要な場合は、
                 * 次のプロバイダーを試す
                 */

Original comment

/* Cache-Control: only-if-cached and revalidation required, try
                 * the next provider
                 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:348

/* 後でアクセスするために古いエントリを退避させる */

Original comment

/* set aside the stale entry for accessing later */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:353

/* 既存の条件式がない場合は、独自の条件式を使用する */

Original comment

/* if no existing conditionals, use conditionals of our own */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:360

/* 私たちの条件と競合する可能性のある既存の条件を削除する */

Original comment

/* Remove existing conditionals that might conflict with ours */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:371-373

/* キャッシュされたetagやLast-Modifiedがある場合は、
                         * 独自の条件を追加する。
                         */

Original comment

/* If we have a cached etag and/or Last-Modified add in
                         * our own conditionals.
                         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:384-388

/*
                         * 独自の条件付きリクエストでRangeリクエストを行わないこと。
                         * 304が返された場合、Rangeは無関係であり、それ以外の場合は
                         * エンティティが変更されているため、完全なエンティティを取得する必要があるため。
                         */

Original comment

/*
                         * Do not do Range requests with our own conditionals: If
                         * we get 304 the Range does not matter and otherwise the
                         * entity changed and we want to have the complete entity
                         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:395

/* 再検証の準備完了、ここには何もなかったことにする */

Original comment

/* ready to revalidate, pretend we were never here */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:399

/* よし、このレスポンスは問題なさそうだ。我々の成果をマージして進めよう。 */

Original comment

/* Okay, this response looks okay.  Merge in our stuff and go. */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:411

/* おっと！エラーです */

Original comment

/* oo-er! an error */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:417

/* Cache-Control: only-if-cached であり、かつキャッシュされていない場合は 504 を返す */

Original comment

/* if Cache-Control: only-if-cached, and not cached, return 504 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:441-443

/*
         * このリクエストの処理中に、以前ここを通過しています。
         */

Original comment

/*
         * We have been here before during the processing of this request.
         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:447-450

/*
     * モジュール設定を取得します。以下の CacheIgnoreQueryString
     * オプションのためにこれが必要です。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Get the module configuration. We need this for the CacheIgnoreQueryString
     * option below.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:454-471

/*
     * キャッシュヒット率を向上させるために正規名を使用しますが、これが
     * プロキシリクエストではない場合、またはリバースプロキシリクエストである場合に限ります。
     * リバースプロキシの場合、以下の状況となるため、両方のケースを同じ方法で処理する必要があります。
     *
     * mod_cacheのクイックハンドラによってキャッシュエントリが検索される際、
     * リバースプロキシの場合、r->proxyreqはまだ設定されていません。これは、
     * クイックハンドラフックの後に実行される（ProxyPassまたはmod_rewriteによる）
     * translate nameフックで設定されるためです。これは、クイックハンドラが
     * 実行される前（post_read_requestフック）に設定されるフォワードプロキシの
     * 場合とは異なります。
     * CACHE_SAVEフィルタによってキャッシュエントリが作成される場合は、
     * 常にr->proxyreqが正しく設定されています。
     * したがって、リバースプロキシの場合でも同じコードパスを使用することを保証する必要があり、
     * リバースプロキシの場合には正規名を使用するのが適切であると考えられます。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Use the canonical name to improve cache hit rate, but only if this is
     * not a proxy request or if this is a reverse proxy request.
     * We need to handle both cases in the same manner as for the reverse proxy
     * case we have the following situation:
     *
     * If a cached entry is looked up by mod_cache's quick handler r->proxyreq
     * is still unset in the reverse proxy case as it only gets set in the
     * translate name hook (either by ProxyPass or mod_rewrite) which is run
     * after the quick handler hook. This is different to the forward proxy
     * case where it gets set before the quick handler is run (in the
     * post_read_request hook).
     * If a cache entry is created by the CACHE_SAVE filter we always have
     * r->proxyreq set correctly.
     * So we must ensure that in the reverse proxy case we use the same code
     * path and using the canonical name seems to be the right thing to do
     * in the reverse proxy case.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:477

/* mod_vhost のように、ホスト名が存在しない場合は _default_ を使用する */

Original comment

/* Use _default_ as the hostname if none present, as in mod_vhost */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:485

/* 解析されたuriのホスト名をコピーする */

Original comment

/* Copy the parsed uri hostname */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:509

/* constの回避策 */

Original comment

/* const work-around */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:492-493

/* プロキシ経由のリクエストであり、ホスト名がありません。
         * 処理が先に進む可能性は低いですが、念のため */

Original comment

/* We are a proxied request, with no hostname. Unlikely
         * to get very far - but just in case */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:497-504

/*
     * スキームをコピーし、小文字であることを確認する。解析された uri に文字列が含まれていない場合、またはこれがプロキシリクエストでない場合は、このリクエストの http スキームを取得する。リバースプロキシリクエストの場合、r->parsed_uri.scheme は設定されないため、「プロキシリクエストなし」と「リバースプロキシリクエスト」の両ケースが同じ方法で処理されることが保証される（これが必要な理由は上記を参照）。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Copy the scheme, ensuring that it is lower case. If the parsed uri
     * contains no string or if this is not a proxy request get the http
     * scheme for this request. As r->parsed_uri.scheme is not set if this
     * is a reverse proxy request, it is ensured that the cases
     * "no proxy request" and "reverse proxy request" are handled in the same
     * manner (see above why this is needed).
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:506

/* スキームをコピーし、小文字に変換する */

Original comment

/* Copy the scheme and lower-case it */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:521-528

/*
     * これがプロキシリクエストであり、かつリバースプロキシリクエストではない場合（これらのケースを同じ方法で処理しなければならない理由については上記のコメントを参照）、URIのポート文字列（サービス名である可能性がある）をコピーする。URIにポート文字列が含まれていない場合は、そのスキームに対するapr-utilのデフォルトポートの概念を使用する（利用可能な場合）。それ以外の場合は、現在のサーバーのポート番号を使用する。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * If this is a proxy request, but not a reverse proxy request (see comment
     * above why these cases must be handled in the same manner), copy the
     * URI's port-string (which may be a service name). If the URI contains
     * no port-string, use apr-util's notion of the default port for that
     * scheme - if available. Otherwise use the port-number of the current
     * server.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:541-545

/* AbsoluteUri にポート文字列が指定されておらず、スキームのデフォルトポートも不明です。
             * 空白のままにして、キャッシュされたエンティティが多少増える非効率さは許容します。
             */

Original comment

/* No port string given in the AbsoluteUri, and we have no
             * idea what the default port for the scheme is. Leave it
             * blank and live with the inefficiency of some extra cached
             * entities.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:557

/* サーバーポートを使用 */

Original comment

/* Use the server port */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:562-565

/*
     * URL内のセッション識別子を無視する必要があるかどうかを確認し、
     * 必要に応じて無視します。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Check if we need to ignore session identifiers in the URL and do so
     * if needed.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:578-581

/*
             * パスの最後のセグメントにパラメータセパレータが存在し、
             * かつパラメータが識別子と一致することを確認する
             */

Original comment

/*
             * Check that we have a parameter separator in the last segment
             * of the path and that the parameter matches our identifier
             */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:589-591

/*
             * 識別子がクエリ文字列に含まれているかを確認し、切り出します。
             */

Original comment

/*
             * Check if the identifier is in the query string and cut it out.
             */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:593-596

/*
                 * まず、識別子がクエリ文字列の先頭にあり、その後に '=' が続いているかを確認する
                 */

Original comment

/*
                 * First check if the identifier is at the beginning of the
                 * query string and followed by a '='
                 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:603-606

/*
                     * サブキーマッチングを回避するため (PR 48401)、
                     * 識別子の先頭に '&' を付加し、末尾に '=' を付加する
                     */

Original comment

/*
                     * In order to avoid subkey matching (PR 48401) prepend
                     * identifier with a '&' and append a '='
                     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:609

/* 何かが見つかった場合、現在 '&' の位置にいる */

Original comment

/* If we found something we are sitting on the '&' */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:630-635

/*
                         * クエリ文字列が "" でない場合、削除した識別子パラメータが
                         * 元のクエリ文字列の最後のものであったということになります。
                         * したがって、末尾に残った '&' を削除する必要があります。
                         */

Original comment

/*
                         * If query string is not "", then we have the case
                         * that the identifier parameter we removed was the
                         * last one in the original query string. Hence we have
                         * a trailing '&' which needs to be removed.
                         */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:645-647

/* キーの形式はURIであり、オプションでクエリ文字列を含まない（conf->ignorequerystringが有効な場合は上記のとおりNULL）
     */

Original comment

/* Key format is a URI, optionally without the query-string (NULL
     * per above if conf->ignorequerystring)
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:648

//", hostname, port_str,

Original comment

//", hostname, port_str,

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:651-658

/*
     * キャッシュ用の request_config にキーを保存する。これは、クイックハンドラによって最初にここを訪れた時点から、CACHE_SAVE フィルタによって2回目に訪れるまでの間に、r->parsed_uri が変更されている可能性があるためである（例: r->parsed_uri がアンエスケープされた場合）。この場合、後続のリクエスト時にクイックハンドラからは決して見つけられないキーでリソースをキャッシュに保存することになってしまう。
     */

Original comment

/*
     * Store the key in the request_config for the cache as r->parsed_uri
     * might have changed in the time from our first visit here triggered by the
     * quick handler and our possible second visit triggered by the CACHE_SAVE
     * filter (e.g. r->parsed_uri got unescaped). In this case we would save the
     * resource in the cache under a key where it is never found by the quick
     * handler during following requests.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:668-671

/* 初期処理（クイックハンドラ、フォワードプロキシ）では、CACHE_SAVE 前の変更がキーに影響を与えないようにするため、r->parsed_uri からの初期クエリ文字列を使用します。それ以外の場合は、実際のクエリ文字列を使用します。
     */

Original comment

/* In early processing (quick-handler, forward proxy), we want the initial
     * query-string from r->parsed_uri, since any change before CACHE_SAVE
     * shouldn't modify the key. Otherwise we want the actual query-string.
     */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:681-689

/*
 * すべてのキャッシュ内の特定のURLエンティティを無効化する
 *
 * このURLのすべてのキャッシュ済みエンティティは、通常POST/PUTまたはDELETEへの応答として削除される。
 *
 * この関数は、少なくとも1つのエンティティが見つかり削除された場合はOKを返し、
 * キャッシュ済みエンティティが削除されなかった場合はDECLINEDを返す。
 */

Original comment

/*
 * Invalidate a specific URL entity in all caches
 *
 * All cached entities for this URL are removed, usually in
 * response to a POST/PUT or DELETE.
 *
 * This function returns OK if at least one entity was found and
 * removed, and DECLINED if no cached entities were removed.
 */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:748

/* キャッシュタイプを順に処理する */

Original comment

/* go through the cache types */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:755

/* リクエストURIを無効化する */

Original comment

/* invalidate the request uri */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:764

/* Location を無効化する */

Original comment

/* invalidate the Location */

Japanese translation

modules/cache/cache_storage.c:775

/* Content-Location を無効化する */

Original comment

/* invalidate the Content-Location */
